summary: Charles Leclerc edged out George Russell in a collision-strewn British Grand Prix at Silverstone, capitalizing on a late safety car restart to secure the win ahead of Lewis Hamilton. Russell mounted a spirited challenge throughout the afternoon while both Max Verstappen and rookie Kimi Antonelli exited the points, unable to recover from early incidents in the chaos. The result marks a significant swing in the championship battle, with Leclerc closing the gap to the lead as Mercedes' home-race hopes slipped away despite Russell's composed driving.
